content: We can borrow the graded reflection teaching framework from high school English vocabulary learning, where reflection is taught as both a noun concept and a repeated active practice, to redesign LLM self-reflection mechanisms. Instead of treating LLM self-reflection as a single post-generation step or a fixed binary check, we can build a dynamic layered reflection routine that reinforces better output over multiple iterations, just like how students deepen their understanding of the word "reflection" through repeated practice across reading, writing and discussion units.
